---
title: "Discrete version"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-27"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# Discrete PPCA + BN learning with combined BIC
# =============================================================================

library(bnlearn)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Discretize a matrix of PC scores into 3 states by tertile thresholds
# Returns a data frame of ordered factors
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
discretize_scores <- function(scores) {
  as.data.frame(lapply(as.data.frame(scores), function(col) {
    cuts <- quantile(col, probs = c(1/3, 2/3))
    cut(col,
        breaks = c(-Inf, cuts, Inf),
        labels = c("low", "mid", "high"),
        ordered_result = FALSE,
        include.lowest = TRUE)
  }))
}


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Reconstruction log-likelihood for one group
# (Gaussian mixture with state-dependent means on W column space)
#
# Uses simplified version: covariance = sigma^2 I (drops within-state variance)
# sigma^2 estimated as mean squared residual over all n*p_g terms
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
recon_loglik <- function(X_group, pca_res, k, disc_scores_g) {
  n  <- nrow(X_group)
  p  <- ncol(X_group)

  # Conditional means of Z given discrete state (empirical within-state means)
  scores_cont <- pca_res$x[, 1:k, drop = FALSE]
  state_means <- sapply(levels(disc_scores_g[[1]]), function(s) {
    NULL  # placeholder -- see below
  })

  # For each observation, get the within-state conditional mean of each PC
  z_tilde <- matrix(0, nrow = n, ncol = k)
  for (j in 1:k) {
    col_j  <- disc_scores_g[[j]]
    cont_j <- scores_cont[, j]
    for (s in levels(col_j)) {
      idx          <- which(col_j == s)
      z_tilde[idx, j] <- mean(cont_j[idx])
    }
  }

  # Reconstructed X using W and z_tilde
  W_k       <- pca_res$rotation[, 1:k, drop = FALSE]
  mu_hat    <- colMeans(X_group)
  X_recon   <- sweep(z_tilde %*% t(W_k), 2, mu_hat, "+")

  # Estimate sigma^2 as mean squared residual
  residuals  <- X_group - X_recon
  sigma2_hat <- mean(residuals^2)

  if (sigma2_hat <= .Machine$double.eps) {
    warning("sigma2_hat near zero in recon_loglik")
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  # Gaussian log-likelihood
  -(n * p / 2) * (log(2 * pi * sigma2_hat) + 1)
}


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Parameter count for reconstruction component
# Loadings + means + sigma^2 + 2 thresholds per PC
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
recon_nparams <- function(p, k) {
  k * p - k * (k - 1) / 2 +   # loadings (corrected for rotation)
  p +                           # group means
  1 +                           # sigma^2
  2 * k                         # discretization thresholds (2 per PC)
}


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compute combined BIC for a given granularity vector
# Returns list with bic_combined and all components
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
compute_combined_bic_disc <- function(data, groups, n_pcs) {
  data <- as.matrix(data)
  n    <- nrow(data)
  g    <- length(groups)

  pca_results  <- vector("list", g)
  disc_list    <- vector("list", g)
  recon_ll     <- numeric(g)
  recon_np     <- integer(g)

  for (j in seq_len(g)) {
    X_j  <- data[, groups[[j]], drop = FALSE]
    k_j  <- n_pcs[j]
    p_j  <- ncol(X_j)

    pca_j          <- prcomp(X_j, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
    pca_results[[j]] <- pca_j

    scores_j       <- pca_j$x[, 1:k_j, drop = FALSE]
    colnames(scores_j) <- paste0("G", j, "_PC", seq_len(k_j))

    disc_j         <- discretize_scores(scores_j)
    disc_list[[j]] <- disc_j

    recon_ll[j]    <- recon_loglik(X_j, pca_j, k_j, disc_j)
    recon_np[j]    <- recon_nparams(p_j, k_j)
  }

  # Assemble discrete BN dataset
  disc_data <- do.call(cbind, disc_list)
  for (col in names(disc_data))
    disc_data[[col]] <- factor(disc_data[[col]], levels = c("low","mid","high"))

  # Learn BN structure via hill-climbing with discrete BIC
  bn_dag    <- hc(disc_data, score = "bic")
  bn_bic    <- score(bn_dag, data = disc_data, type = "bic")

  # Recover BN log-likelihood and parameter count from bnlearn BIC
  # bnlearn BIC = loglik - log(n)/2 * nparams
  # => need logLik separately
  bn_fit    <- bn.fit(bn_dag, disc_data)
  bn_ll     <- logLik(bn_fit, data = disc_data)
  bn_np     <- round(2 * (bn_ll - bn_bic) / log(n))

  total_ll  <- bn_ll + sum(recon_ll)
  total_np  <- bn_np + sum(recon_np)
  bic_comb  <- total_ll - (log(n) / 2) * total_np

  list(
    bn_dag       = bn_dag,
    bn_fit       = bn_fit,
    disc_data    = disc_data,
    bn_loglik    = bn_ll,
    bn_nparams   = bn_np,
    recon_ll     = recon_ll,
    recon_np     = recon_np,
    total_loglik = total_ll,
    total_nparams = total_np,
    bic_combined = bic_comb,
    n_pcs        = n_pcs
  )
}


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Coordinate ascent algorithm
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
learn_disc_bn_pca <- function(data,
                               groups,
                               max_iter    = 20,
                               n_restarts  = 3,
                               verbose     = TRUE) {
  data   <- as.matrix(data)
  g      <- length(groups)
  p_grps <- sapply(groups, length)
  k_max  <- p_grps - 1L   # upper bound per group

  best_global     <- NULL
  best_global_bic <- -Inf

  for (restart in seq_len(n_restarts)) {

    # Initialise: random k_g in {1, ..., min(3, k_max_g)}
    k_curr <- sapply(k_max, function(km) sample(1:min(3, km), 1))
    res    <- compute_combined_bic_disc(data, groups, k_curr)
    bic_curr <- res$bic_combined

    if (verbose)
      cat(sprintf("\n--- Restart %d | Init k=(%s) | BIC=%.2f\n",
                  restart, paste(k_curr, collapse=","), bic_curr))

    for (iter in seq_len(max_iter)) {
      improved <- FALSE

      for (j in seq_len(g)) {

        # Candidate values: k_j - 1, k_j + 1 (current is already evaluated)
        candidates <- setdiff(
          c(k_curr[j] - 1L, k_curr[j] + 1L),
          c(0L, k_max[j] + 1L)
        )

        for (k_try in candidates) {
          k_new      <- k_curr
          k_new[j]   <- k_try
          res_try    <- compute_combined_bic_disc(data, groups, k_new)
          bic_try    <- res_try$bic_combined

          if (verbose)
            cat(sprintf("  Iter %d | Group %d | k=(%s) | BIC=%.2f %s\n",
                        iter, j,
                        paste(k_new, collapse=","),
                        bic_try,
                        ifelse(bic_try > bic_curr, "<-- improved", "")))

          if (bic_try > bic_curr) {
            k_curr   <- k_new
            bic_curr <- bic_try
            res      <- res_try
            improved <- TRUE
          }
        }
      }

      if (!improved) {
        if (verbose) cat(sprintf("  Converged at iter %d.\n", iter))
        break
      }
    }

    if (bic_curr > best_global_bic) {
      best_global_bic <- bic_curr
      best_global     <- res
    }
  }

  if (verbose) {
    cat(sprintf("\n=== Best solution across %d restarts ===\n", n_restarts))
    cat(sprintf("  k = (%s)\n", paste(best_global$n_pcs, collapse=",")))
    cat(sprintf("  BIC_combined = %.3f\n", best_global_bic))
  }

  best_global
}


# =============================================================================
# Example usage on synthetic data from check 6
# =============================================================================
set.seed(99)
n6     <- 500
Z1     <- cbind(rnorm(n6), rnorm(n6))
Z2     <- cbind(0.7 * Z1[,1] + rnorm(n6, sd=0.5))
Z3     <- cbind(0.6 * Z2[,1] + rnorm(n6, sd=0.5), rnorm(n6))

make_group_data <- function(Z, p, sigma2) {
  k  <- ncol(Z); W  <- matrix(rnorm(p*k), nrow=p); mu <- rnorm(p)
  E  <- matrix(rnorm(nrow(Z)*p, sd=sqrt(sigma2)), nrow=nrow(Z))
  sweep(Z %*% t(W) + E, 2, mu, "+")
}

X1     <- make_group_data(Z1, 5, 0.3)
X2     <- make_group_data(Z2, 4, 0.4)
X3     <- make_group_data(Z3, 4, 0.3)
data6  <- cbind(X1, X2, X3)
groups6 <- list(1:5, 6:9, 10:13)

result_disc <- learn_disc_bn_pca(data6, groups6,
                                  max_iter   = 20,
                                  n_restarts = 3,
                                  verbose    = TRUE)

cat("\nLearned BN structure:\n")
print(result_disc$bn_dag)
```
```{r}
# Pretty-printed summary of all nodes ---
for (node in names(result_disc$bn_fit)) {
  cat(sprintf("\n=== Node: %s ===\n", node))
  print(result_disc$bn_fit[[node]]$prob)
}
```
```{r}
print_obs_conditionals <- function(data, groups, result) {
  data  <- as.matrix(data)
  n_pcs <- result$n_pcs
  g     <- length(groups)

  # Fix 1: ensure factor levels are correct regardless of how cbind stored them
  disc_data <- result$disc_data
  for (col in names(disc_data))
    disc_data[[col]] <- factor(as.character(disc_data[[col]]),
                               levels = c("low", "mid", "high"))

  for (j in seq_len(g)) {
    X_j   <- data[, groups[[j]], drop = FALSE]
    k_j   <- n_pcs[j]
    p_j   <- ncol(X_j)
    n     <- nrow(X_j)

    cat(sprintf("\n%s\n", strrep("=", 60)))
    cat(sprintf("Group %d | %d observed variables | %d PC(s)\n", j, p_j, k_j))
    cat(sprintf("%s\n", strrep("=", 60)))

    pca_j    <- prcomp(X_j, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
    W_j      <- pca_j$rotation[, 1:k_j, drop = FALSE]
    mu_j     <- colMeans(X_j)
    scores_j <- pca_j$x[, 1:k_j, drop = FALSE]

    disc_cols <- grep(sprintf("^G%d_PC", j), names(disc_data), value = TRUE)
    disc_j    <- disc_data[, disc_cols, drop = FALSE]

    state_levels <- c("low", "mid", "high")

    # Compute within-state conditional means for each PC
    z_tilde_list <- vector("list", k_j)
    for (pc in seq_len(k_j)) {
      z_tilde_list[[pc]] <- sapply(state_levels, function(s) {
        idx <- which(as.character(disc_j[[pc]]) == s)  # Fix 1: use as.character
        if (length(idx) == 0) NA_real_ else mean(scores_j[idx, pc])
      })
    }

    # Compute sigma^2 using within-state means
    z_tilde_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = n, ncol = k_j)
    for (pc in seq_len(k_j)) {
      for (s in state_levels) {
        idx <- which(as.character(disc_j[[pc]]) == s)
        if (length(idx) > 0)
          z_tilde_mat[idx, pc] <- z_tilde_list[[pc]][s]
      }
    }
    X_recon    <- sweep(z_tilde_mat %*% t(W_j), 2, mu_j, "+")
    sigma2_hat <- mean((X_j - X_recon)^2)

    cat(sprintf("Shared noise variance sigma^2 = %.4f  (sd = %.4f)\n\n",
                sigma2_hat, sqrt(sigma2_hat)))

    # Fix 3: unname values to avoid named-scalar column naming issues
    cat("Within-state conditional means of PC scores:\n")
    z_tilde_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(k_j), function(pc) {
      data.frame(
        PC   = sprintf("G%d_PC%d", j, pc),
        low  = round(unname(z_tilde_list[[pc]]["low"]),  4),
        mid  = round(unname(z_tilde_list[[pc]]["mid"]),  4),
        high = round(unname(z_tilde_list[[pc]]["high"]), 4),
        row.names = NULL
      )
    }))
    print(z_tilde_df, row.names = FALSE)

    # Print conditional mean vectors for each state combination
    state_grid <- expand.grid(replicate(k_j, state_levels, simplify = FALSE),
                              stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    names(state_grid) <- sprintf("G%d_PC%d", j, seq_len(k_j))

    cat(sprintf("\nConditional mean vectors E[X_g | states]  (%d combinations):\n",
                nrow(state_grid)))

    for (r in seq_len(nrow(state_grid))) {
      # Fix 2: extract state labels column by column, not with as.character on a row
      state_vec <- sapply(seq_len(k_j), function(pc) state_grid[r, pc])
      z_vec     <- sapply(seq_len(k_j), function(pc) z_tilde_list[[pc]][state_vec[pc]])
      cond_mean <- mu_j + W_j %*% z_vec

      state_label <- paste(sprintf("G%d_PC%d=%s", j, seq_len(k_j), state_vec),
                           collapse = ", ")
      cat(sprintf("\n  %s\n", state_label))
      cat(sprintf("  E[X_%d] = %s\n", j,
                  paste(round(cond_mean, 4), collapse = ", ")))
    }
  }
}

# Usage
print_obs_conditionals(data6, groups6, result_disc)
```
